pins.description: The DAO was exploited in June 2016 through a reentrancy vulnerability, leading to the loss of about 3.6 million ETH ($60 million USD). The incident triggered Ethereum’s hard fork into ETH and ETC and marked a turning point for smart contract auditing and the growth of the blockchain security industry.
Bitcoin’s 2nd halving, the block reward was cut from 25 BTC to 12.5 BTC
Bitcoin’s 3rd halving, the block reward was cut from 12.5 BTC to 6.25 BTC

The launch of the Compound token (COMP) is considered the start of DeFi Summer, when on-chain finance rapidly scaled and security concerns gradually shifted from isolated smart contract bugs to systemic and economic risks.
TerraUSD lost its dollar peg and triggered a death spiral in LUNA, wiping out much of the ecosystem's value within days and intensifying the broader crypto market sell-off.
Bitcoin’s 4th halving, the block reward was cut from 6.25 BTC to 3.125 BTC

OpenClaw was officially released/renamed, marking a shift from chat-based AI toward agentic task execution. Its release signaled to the ICT industry that AI agents were becoming a practical software layer for browser control, tool orchestration, and cross-system operations.

A total of 61 DeFi incidents occurred within a single month, marking the highest monthly level since October 2022. Meanwhile, the releases of Opus 4.7 and GPT 5.5 in the middle of the month further highlighted the trend of AI significantly lowering the cost of executing DeFi attacks.

DPRK APT group UNC4736 spent 6 months conducting social engineering operations, posing as a quantitative trading firm to gain the trust of Drift Protocol's contributors. The attackers compromised a signer’s device, obtained pre-signed multisig transactions, and drained $285 million within 128 seconds. It became the largest DeFi exploit of 2026.
